concepts.finding:
In summary, our experiments aim to answer
the following three questions:
• Q1: Does our proposed Hubble System outperform other
state-of-the-art approaches in the audience expansion task?
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
From Table 3, we can observe that the proposed Hubble System
maintains a great improvement on the number of conversion and
CVR, as well as slightly improvement in the number of exposure,
against the MLP classifier.
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

concepts.finding:
In summary, the proposed Hubble System performs
more effectively and efficiently in the real-world online A/B testing,
thus is more suitable for the industry.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Second, conventional two-stage audience expansion
systems neglect the high-order user-campaign interactions and
usually generate entangled user embeddings, thus fail to achieve
high-quality user representation.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Specifically, in the offline stage, we propose a novel
adaptive and disentangled graph neural network (called AD-GNN),
to adaptively explore the user-campaign graph and generate com-
prehensive user embedding in a disentangled manner.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Considering the fact that some users may have similar prefer-
ences toward campaigns, we propose to divide the seeds into sev-
eral groups according to their preference, which is encoded in user
embedding, and calculate the similarity between user groups and
past campaigns.
concepts.implication: Can inform intent modeling for recommendation systems

concepts.finding:
For each cluster, we find the most similar past cam-
paign based on the similarity between its cluster embedding and all
past campaign embeddings.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

4
EXPERIMENTS
3 In this section, we conduct extensive offline and online experi-
ments on the proposed Hubble System, to demonstrate its effective-
ness and efficiency.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Before diving into the experimental results, we first introduce the
experiment settings, including datasets, comparison methods and
evaluation metrics.

concepts.finding:
Meth-
ods in the latter category propose to expand audiences based on
user embeddings generated by a machine learning model, and have
achieved success in some companies (e.g., Linkedin [7], Pinterest [1]
and Tencent [11]).
concepts.implication: Can inform intent modeling for recommendation systems

concepts.finding:
For the offline stage, we propose a novel GNN model, called
AD-GNN, to adaptively explore the user-campaign graph and gener-
ate high-quality user embedding in a disentangled manner.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
For the
online stage, we propose a novel KD-AE model, which employs a
well-designed knowledge distillation mechanism, to absorb knowl-
edge from AD-GNN and eliminate the coverage bias introduced
by the given seed users.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
By combining these two stages, Hubble
System demonstrates its superior effectiveness and efficiency in
both offline experiments and online A/B testing, compared to other
state-of-the-art approaches.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation
